GIDA Digital Assessment Dashboard

Growth-Informed Development Assessment | Kindergarten Farms Assessment Series

Local HTML prototype for teacher-administered scoring, notes, and printable reports.

Disclaimer: This digital tool is a student-created academic prototype. It has not been field-tested, psychometrically validated, reliability tested, or peer reviewed. It is not intended for grading, placement, language proficiency determination, special education referral, or program evaluation.

Student Profile

Packet 1: Diagnostic Content and Language Inventory

52 points Baseline knowledge, vocabulary, and oral language data.

Part A: Farm Knowledge Interview

Use CKLA image 1A-1. Score each question 0–4.

CKLA Visual: 1A-1 Farm SceneUse licensed instructional image during administration.
QuestionScoreNotes / Verbatim Response
What do you see?
What do you think happens on a farm?
What kinds of animals might live on a farm?
What do farmers do?
Where does food come from?

Part B: Farm Vocabulary Identification

1 point per correct identification.

Part C: Farm Animal Description Task

Prompt: “Tell me something you know about this animal.” Follow-up: “What else can you tell me?” Score each 0–4.

AnimalCKLA VisualScoreNotes
Cow2A-3
Chicken3A-2
Pig4A-4
Sheep5A-1

Part D: Farm Representation Task

Students draw a farm; no independent labels or written sentences are expected.

Packet 1 Score

Total: — / 52
Interpretation: —

Packet 2: Academic Language Assessment

68 points Formative language, vocabulary, and content communication.

Part A: Listening Comprehension

1 point per correct response.

Part B: Speaking Assessment

For each image ask: “What do you see?” “What is happening?” “Why is this important?”

ImageLanguage 0–4Content 0–2Notes
Cow image
Chicken image
Pig image
Sheep image

Part C: Vocabulary Recognition and Comprehension

TargetRecognition 0–1Comprehension 0–2Notes
Herd
Flock
Shelter
Grazing
Hatch
Peck

Part D: Academic Language Production

Score each prompt 0–4 using the packet rubric.

PromptScoreNotes
This is a ____.
The ____ lives in a ____.
The farmer takes care of the ____.
The ____ needs ____.
